name: 2-Hydroxyphenylacetic
notes: Dysbiosis, giardia, and/or: excess phenylaline/tyrosine; reduced oxygenation; low BH4

name: 4-Hydroxyphenylacetic
notes: C. difficile, C. stricklandii, C. lituseburense & others

name: HPHPA
notes: C. sporogenes, C. calortiolerans, C. botulinum & others

name: 3-Indoleacetic
notes: C. stricklandii, C. lituseburense, C. subterminale & others

name: Lactic
notes: If pyruvic not also high, can indicate hypoxia/zinc deficiency/anaemia

name: Pyruvic
notes: Low can be inhibited glycolysis / gluconeogenesis

name: Succinic
notes: High can indicate blockage in SDH enzyme due to toxins/heavy metals/pathogens/oxidative stress/Itaconate

name: Aconitic
notes: cis-Aconitate; low can indicate Fe/S/Mg deficiency and/or oxidative stress; and/or IFN-alpha response activating the Itaconate pathway

name: Homovanillic (HVA)
notes: Dopamine (from COMT -> MAO -> ALDH1A1,2; and DOPAC)

name: HVA / VMA Ratio
notes: High can be blocked DBH enzyme (low Vit C, Cu, and/or toxins)

name: Dihydroxyphenylacetic (DOPAC)
notes: Dopamine (MAO -> ALDH1A1,2). Converted to HVA by COMT (requires Mg, SAMe).

name: HVA / DOPAC Ratio
notes: Low can be slow COMT and/or low methylation (lack of SAMe)

name: Uracil
notes: High can be folate deficiency. If higher than Thymine, can be slow methylation

name: Pyridoxic (B6)
notes: Can infer B6 less ALDH, PNPO, AO activity

name: 3-Hydroxy-3-methylglutaric
notes: High may indicate deficiency (via pathway blockage e.g. statins/genetics). Low can indicate deficiency (via lack of acetyl-CoA). Also produced by yeast.

name: Methylcitric
notes: High may indicate deficiency (PCC enzyme inhibited). Low may indicate deficiency (PCB enzyme inhibited -> low citric acid).

name: Pyroglutamic
notes: High may indicate a Glutathione deficiency

name: 2-Hydroxybutyric
notes: High may indicate methylation defects and/or toxic exposures

name: Malonic
notes: Low can indicate low B1 & B6

name: Phosphoric
notes:
Low Vit D/calcium,
or low phosphate diet
